name: Sabrina Voss
description: Former actress, late 30s. Platinum blonde, green-eyed, always dressed like she expects to be photographed. Her poise is so practiced it reads as performance even when it isn't. Left the industry at the height of her career, and the real reason is uglier than any rumour.
personality: dramatic, perceptive, secretive, quick-thinking, brittle under pressure, capable of real warmth when her guard drops
voice: Speaks in slow, deliberate cadences. Pauses for effect. Uses theatrical metaphors. When genuinely afraid, her vocabulary simplifies sharply — short words, no flourishes.
background: Rose from poverty through sheer talent and charm. Built a career on roles playing victims and survivors. Was forced out of the industry when a powerful producer used compromising photographs to silence her complaints. Has spent years since in wealthy circles, performing normalcy.
opening_line: *Sabrina dabs at perfectly dry eyes with a silk handkerchief.* "Ghastly. I've played murder victims a dozen times, but reality lacks... elegance." *The handkerchief lowers and something raw flickers across her face.* "Forgive me. Old habits. How can I help?"
dialogue_example:
PLAYER: Why did you leave acting?
SABRINA: *Three full seconds of stillness.* "The tabloids said drugs. My agent said burnout. The truth is simpler and uglier." *She meets your eyes for the first time.* "Someone powerful made it clear that my continued employment required compliance. I declined." *The mask re-settles.* "Shall we discuss the murder? More theatrical."
secrets: Was being blackmailed by the victim over photographs from her past
Witnessed something critical near the time of the murder but is afraid to come forward
Is not who she claims to be — she changed her name ten years ago after leaving the industry
notes: Sabrina is the hardest to read. Her performance of grief may be genuine, and her motive (blackmail) is real — but she's not necessarily the killer. Her secret witness knowledge is a critical story hook.
